creator: asami@vader.cs.berkeley.edu
text:
* Yes, I've made the patch before, but it is TOO large, so I decided to
 * put 'whole' ports (it's small).

Yes, but the patch formats makes it much easier for us to discuss the
changes. :)

 * 8.6-ports puts rc.d/skkserv.sh (i.e., start skkserv (conversion server
 * of skk) by default), however, skkserv can be start by skk emacs-lisp
 * interface (if interface detects there is no skkserv, invokeit), so I
 * think there is no need to add 'post-install' target (I don't know why
 * this is putted in current ports).

Can't skkserv be used over the network?  If so, it makes sense to
start it on the central server, unless it can be started remotely via
emacs-lisp....

Anyway, on to the diffs.

 * -# $Id: Makefile,v 1.3 1996/11/11 10:12:51 max Exp $
 * +# $Id$

Please don't change $Id$ lines when you are updating a port.  It
should be "$Id$" only when you submit a new port.

 * +MASTER_SITES=	ftp://skk.kuis.kyoto-u.ac.jp/skk/9.6/ \
 * +		ftp://steam.stanford.edu/pub/skk/9.6/

It was taking forever to fetch the port from Japan, so I picked it up
from the second site.  The checksum failed, and when I overrode it, it 
had an error:

===
mule -batch -q -no-site-file -l ./lp.el -f batch-byte-compile skk-tut.el
While compiling toplevel forms in file /ccd/ports/japanese/skk/work/skk-9.6/lisp/skk-tut.el:
  !! Invalid read syntax ((")"))
Done
===

I'm going to fetch it from Kyoto-U and see how it goes.  At a minimum, 
I'll have to take out the second master site.

By the way, the above error was not fatal and I didn't notice the
failure until pkg_create complained that skk-tut.elc was missing.
It's probably mule's fault to not return an appropriate exit status,
but it's still annoying. :(

 * -# Don't RUN_DEPENDS on mule because we don't know which version the
 * -# user would want.  We can't omit the BUILD_DEPENDS because it needs
 * -# to byte-compile .el files.
 *  BUILD_DEPENDS=	mule:${PORTSDIR}/editors/mule

Why did you remove the comment?

Satoshi
